_article: XXXIX.2 — herbs continue to ripen. XXXIX.5 — a half-harvested herb garden is a canonical perceivable-momentum cue. XXXIX.6 — a ripening herb can be harvested early (losing potency) or guarded (gaining potency but risking discovery).
_description: Momentum schema for a FloraPatch / SpiritHerbCluster entity. Herbs are not static loot drops. They grow, ripen, gain potency, decay if not harvested, mutate under spirit-density fluctuations, and can be harvested early (low potency) or guarded to maturity (high potency but risk another forager finding them). A half-harvested patch is a live trajectory — the cultivator who was picking here will return.

_design_notes.why_this_matters: Er Gen canon is full of spirit herbs that must be harvested at the right moment. The player who arrives at a patch mid-ripening faces a real choice: take it now (weak), wait (risk losing it to the returning cultivator or a beast), or guard it (reveal presence). This is perceivable, unannounced, consequential.
_design_notes.coupling: Fed by: spirit_vein_momentum (qi density drives growth rate and potency ceiling), beast_population (beasts may graze or destroy patches). Feeds: economy_momentum (rare herb supply), npc motivation (a cultivator's herb-gathering motivation is satisfied by harvesting).
perceivable_without_notification_example: A spirit herb patch with some plants picked and others left — someone was here and will return.
A herb that the village herbalist calls 'barely budding' is in fact days from maturity — perceivable by a cultivator who knows herbs.
Tracks near a rare patch — a forager or a beast has been here recently.
The patch is healthier than the surrounding flora — it sits on a spirit vein node (qi density visible to spiritual sense).

schema.patch_id: string — matches a FloraPatch / SpiritHerbCluster entity id

current_state.species_id: string — matches a flora/canon species id
current_state.plant_count: int — number of individual plants in the patch
current_state.potency: float 0.0–1.0 — current medicinal/spiritual potency (rises with ripeness to a peak, then decays)
current_state.maturation_eta_ticks: int — ticks until peak maturity at current growth rate
current_state.decay_eta_ticks: int — ticks until potency drops below usable threshold after peak (null if not yet mature)
current_state.mutation_state: enum — stable | mutating | mutated — spirit-density surges or elemental events may mutate the patch
current_state.harvest_history: array — {tick, harvester_id, count_taken} — who has harvested and when (the 'half-harvested' cue comes from a non-empty recent history)

rate_of_change.ripeness_per_tick: float — driven by (spirit_vein qi density, soil quality, weather, season, species base rate)
rate_of_change.potency_per_tick: float — rises with ripeness to peak, then falls; mutation can raise the ceiling

components.potency_accumulation: float — rises toward ceiling; ceiling set by spirit_density and species
components.decay_rate: float — after peak, potency decays at species-specific rate
components.mutation_pressure: float — chance per tick of mutation (raised by spirit-density surges, elemental events)

drivers: string — e.g. 'crimson_root_patch_west: species crimson_root, 12 plants, ripeness 0.62, potency 0.55, maturation_eta 8000 ticks, spirit_vein_west qi_density 0.58 (adequate), last harvested 3 days ago by npc_wang_lin (took 4 of 16 — half-harvested cue)'

thresholds.name: peak_maturity
thresholds.consequence: patch is at peak; harvest now for maximum potency. If unharvested, decay_eta countdown begins. This is the harvest window.

thresholds.name: overripening
thresholds.consequence: potency begins to decay; the patch is wasting. A cultivator who waited too long loses value. If left, the patch may drop seeds (founding a new patch next season) and the current generation becomes unusable.

thresholds.name: mutation
thresholds.condition: mutation_pressure threshold crossed (spirit surge or elemental event)
thresholds.consequence: patch mutates; species_id may shift to a variant; potency ceiling and properties change; the patch becomes a different resource. Canon-grade spirit herb mutations are rare and valuable.

thresholds.name: depletion
thresholds.consequence: patch cannot sustain itself; over-harvesting has killed it. The patch is removed from the compiled state; the dependent economy/cultivation opportunity is lost until a new patch seeds (seasons to years).

interruptible_by: Harvest (reduces plant_count and ripeness; may end the trajectory if depleted)
Guarding (player or NPC stays near; raises chance of catching a competing forager; Art XXXIX.6)
Spirit vein surge (raises growth_rate and potency ceiling; may trigger mutation)
Beast grazing (reduces plant_count; may end trajectory)
Formation placed to accelerate growth or raise potency ceiling
schema.perceivable_without_notification: string — REQUIRED (Art XXXIX.5). e.g. 'A cluster of crimson root, half-picked — four stalks cut cleanly, eight still growing, the cut marks less than three days old. Someone will return. The roots are budding but not yet flowered — a cultivator who knows herbs knows they are ten days from peak.' If empty, FAILS the Momentum Test.
schema.compiled_into: settlement_graph (resource node); feeds economy_momentum; coupled to spirit_graph (qi dependency)
schema.canon_ref: EXPLICIT — spirit herbs and their harvest timing are pervasive Er Gen canon; specific patches INFERRED (Art XV) for Chapter 1 geography
